title: Multifunctional self-driven origami paper-based integrated microfluidic chip to detect CRP and PAB in whole blood
hypothesis: A self-driven paper-based microfluidic biosensor functionalized for C-reactive protein detection will quantify CRP directly from whole blood without preprocessing in a point-of-care workflow, while preserving clinically useful sensitivity and turnaround time.
notes: Strong test of literature QC and point-of-care materials grounding.

title: An optimised electrochemical biosensor for the label-free detection of C-reactive protein in blood
hypothesis: An optimized label-free electrochemical immunosensor using anti-CRP capture chemistry on gold electrodes will detect C-reactive protein in blood with clinically relevant sensitivity and specificity suitable for translational assay development.
notes: Good control for electrochemical supplier and validation realism.

title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG treatment improves intestinal permeability and modulates microbiota dysbiosis in an experimental model of sepsis
hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for four weeks before challenge will reduce intestinal permeability relative to controls, measurable by FITC-dextran translocation and tight-junction protein preservation.
notes: Benchmark for animal-study controls, timeline realism, and biological validation.

title: Freezing-induced uptake of trehalose into mammalian cells facilitates cryopreservation
hypothesis: Replacing conventional sugar additives with trehalose in a mammalian-cell freezing workflow will improve post-thaw survival by facilitating intracellular protection during freezing and thawing compared with standard cryopreservation conditions.
notes: Useful for detecting whether references stay on-domain or drift into unrelated cryobiology.

title: Performance of different Sporomusa species for the microbial electrosynthesis of acetate from carbon dioxide
hypothesis: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical microbial electrosynthesis system will convert CO2 into acetate at a higher rate than alternative Sporomusa species under controlled cathode-driven conditions.
notes: Hardest benchmark for domain-specific grounding and realistic reactor planning.
